note:
关键字：Christopher Mims剧作家KarelČapek创造了“机器人”一词后的一个世纪，我们终于有了使科幻小说成为现实的技术-越来越好
   1921年1月25日，卡雷尔·卡佩克（KarelČapek）的剧作“ R.U.R。”（“罗斯森的通用机器人”的简称）在布拉格首映。这是一种感觉。在不到两年的时间内，它已被翻译成30种语言，包括英语，并在其中引入了“机器人”一词。 Čapek的不怀好意的人类奴隶注定要崛起并摧毁他们的制造者的愿景，从此塑造了我们对自动化和我们自己的看法。
  在虚构的机器人和实际的机器人的发明者之间长达一个世纪的对话中，工程师大多被迫追赶，实现或颠覆最初在书籍，电影和电视中阐述的机器人的视野。
  现在，在某些领域，机器人的现实已经超越了小说，甚至领先于那些以学习机器人为生的人。
  在小说，工业和休闲领域，我们的自动化伴侣每年都在变得越来越复杂和广泛。
    希瑟·奈特（Heather Knight）是一名工程师，是“社交机器人专家”，也是俄勒冈州立大学机器人计划的13个核心教师之一。 10月下旬的一天，她震惊地发现校园里满是由星舰机器人公司（Starship Robotics）制造的无人驾驶六轮汽车车队。这家位于旧金山的公司已与校园餐饮服务公司签约，以提供非接触式交付。
    她补充说：“我们现在甚至连机器人技术人员都不知道校园里会有机器人。” 
机器人的这种新可见性-现在在商店，酒店和医疗保健设施中，以及在我们的街道和头顶上方-都表明了它们的发展性质。这也是分水岭时刻的外部标志。
  根据非营利性行业组织国际机器人联合会（International Federation of Robotics）的数据，2019年售出并使用了37.3万台工业机器人，该组织根据供应商数据进行年度全球机器人普查。自2014年以来，这一数字每年以11％的速度增长，全球共有270万台工业机器人在使用。工业机器人是1961年首次安装在通用汽车工厂的Unimate机械臂的后代，在制造，执行焊接，喷漆和组装等任务中很常见。他们努力工作，但不是很聪明。
  根据该联合会的数据，同样在2019年，售出并安装了173,000台“专业服务机器人”。到2023年，这个数字预计每年将达到53.7万台，增长三倍。这是企业在制造领域以外使用的机器人。它们执行各种功能，包括国防，仓库自动化和医院消毒。
    这些机器人往往更智能，配备了先进的软件，传感器和Wi-Fi或其他形式的连接。他们通常可以与人并肩工作，而不是像在工业机器人那样的工厂里躲藏，而不是在禁止人员进出的围墙区域。
  如果这两种类型的机器人的当前增长率保持不变，并且我们将不包括机器人联合会在内的专业服务型机器人（例如仅由公司自己生产的公司所包括的机器人）纳入考虑范围，那么在未来一年左右的时间里服务机器人将超过已售或已安装的工业机器人。这种趋势将为公司和消费者带来新的利益，并为工人带来新的挑战。
  到目前为止，专业服务机器人的最大份额是用于物流的机器人。于2003年创立Kiva Systems的Mick Mountz帮助开拓了软件，连接性和传感器的使用方法，再加上诸如电动机，变速箱，电池和轮胎之类的现成零件，创造出了价格相对可负担的机器人，它比他们的前辈，工业机器人。
  Mountz先生说：“如今的自动化与50或60年前的自动化之间的主要区别在于，我们增加了软件。”他补充说，至关重要的是无线连接性（当时Wi-Fi是新技术）和现成的传感器，例如原始Kiva机器人中使用的黑白相机。 
亚马逊在2012年收购了Kiva，并成为了Amazon Robotics。 Kiva的机器人和软件仍然可能是当今最著名的服务机器人示例的基础：亚马逊在其仓库中使用的滚动的披萨盒形“驱动单元”，将商品货架移交给负责拣选和包装客户货物的人员订单。
    Mountz先生补充说，从运输到制造业，我们工业界的许多领域尚未受到新型自动化和机器人技术的影响，但可能会受到影响。
  得益于德克萨斯州农工大学人道主义机器人与人工智能实验室主任罗宾·墨菲（Robin Murphy）进行的国家科学基金会资助的研究，事实证明，新一代机器人已经能够适应多种任务。在全球大流行的早期，Murphy博士及其团队着手研究如何使用机器人来帮助人类适应Covid-19的影响。她和她的团队记录了326种不同的机器人，这些机器人用于29种不同的应用，远程医疗和医院消毒，以隔离检疫，交付，远程呈现，建筑，农业，物流和实验室自动化。
  墨菲博士说，其中有87％是现有的机器人，它们适合于应对新病毒。她补充说，可用于抵抗大流行的庞大数量和种类繁多的成熟机器人技术表明，如今公司和组织如何选择机器人来宠爱。
  俄勒冈州立大学的奈特博士说，这种大流行病可能会加速机器人的采用。尽管人类通常不愿改变，但战争和自然灾害会激发人们的快速变化。她说：“创新的方式不同，但是对必要性的回应是影响最大的方式之一。”
  分析人员和数据收集者没有将所有事物视为机器人，这说明了机器人技术已变得如此广泛，以及如今日常生活中可以充满自主性的事物。完整的账目可能包括美国联邦航空管理局最近授权在美国飞行的所有无人驾驶飞机，Waymo及其竞争对手生产的许多自动驾驶汽车，小型轮式送货机器人的快速增长，跨海自动驾驶船，数以千万计的机器人真空吸尘器，半个世纪的无人航天器，以及随着它们变得越来越互联和“更智能”，甚至我们的家园。
    在2019年，世界上第一家“机器人旅馆”被迫淘汰243个机器人中的一半以上，因为它们使客人和同事的生活更加困难。沃尔玛在发现人类可以更有效地完成工作后，于2020年11月取消了让机器人清点商店货架的计划。事实证明，机器人似乎更适合执行许多任务，而这些任务超出了他们仍然相当有限的认知能力，从而迫使公司雇用人员来进行远程驾驶。 
布鲁金斯学会高级研究员马克·穆罗说，尽管存在这些棘手的问题，但机器人和自动化挑战美国工人的步伐可能会加快。自动化一直以来对蓝领工人的生活造成的破坏性最大。但是随着机器人从在工厂转移人员到在服务业转移人员的服务转变（在美国，服务业的规模是制造业的许多倍），这可能会影响数百万人的生活。
  根据Muro先生和布鲁金斯大学的其他人进行的历史分析，像我们所经历的那样的衰退是企业最有可能用自动化代替人类的时代。发生这种情况的原因是，当经济衰退袭来时，收入的下降速度要快于工资的下降速度。结果是，对于现金短缺的公司来说，自动化已经从精巧的工作变成了必要的工作。
  Muro先生补充说，即使经济复苏，这种自动化也不会消失。从长远来看，自动化可以提高经济生产率并创造更多的就业机会，但就短期而言，这可能意味着失业和失业的人会失业。尽管自动化的生产率提高了，但美国仍面临着越来越严重的经济不平等现象，这是一个长期而恶化的问题。
    奈特博士说，十年前当她发表演讲时，她告诉听众，机器人革命已经在进行中，只有在工厂和仓库之类的秘密场所才发生。现在不同的是，机器人革命是在公共场合发生的，因此是不可避免的，甚至是个人的。在我们的家中，我们的工作场所，我们的街道上，我们的天空中，机器人正成为我们日常生活的一部分，这是前所未有的。
  Čapek在英语中引入了机器人一词之后的一个世纪，现实生活中机器人所要做的一件事就是横行无声，摧毁我们所有人，就像他们在他的剧本和无数科幻作品中所做的那样。但是他确实做对了一件事情：随着他们队伍的扩大，以及他们在更多地方执行更多任务，机器人正以自己的方式接管工作。
  在2021年1月23日的印刷版中显示为“机器人的现实冲刺过去的小说”。
note_en:
Keywords: Christopher Mims     A century after playwright Karel Čapek coined the word ‘robot,’ we finally have the technology to make the stuff of science fiction a reality—for better and for worse
   On Jan. 25, 1921, Karel Čapek’s play “R.U.R.”—short for “Rossum’s Universal Robots”— premiered in Prague. It was a sensation. Within two years it had been translated into 30 languages, including English, to which it introduced the word “robot.” Čapek’s vision of unwilling slaves of humanity destined to rise up and destroy their makers has  shaped our view of both automation and ourselves ever since.
  In a century-long dialogue between inventors of fictional and actual robots, engineers have for the most part been forced to play catch-up, either realizing or subverting the vision of robots first expounded in books, movies and television.
  Now, the reality of robots is in some areas running ahead of fiction, even ahead of what those who study robots for a living are able to keep track of.
  In fiction, industry and leisure, our automated companions have grown more sophisticated and widespread with every passing year.
    Heather Knight is an engineer, “social roboticist” and one of 13 core faculty in Oregon State University’s robotics program. One day in late October, she was shocked to find the campus crawling with a fleet of autonomous, six-wheeled vehicles made by  Starship Robotics. The San Francisco-based company had  contracted with the campus dining service to provide contactless delivery.
    “We’re at the point where not even the people in robotics know there are going to be robots on campus,” she adds.
  This new visibility of robots—now in  stores,  hotels and  health-care facilities, as well as  on our streets and  above our heads—is an indicator of their evolving nature. It’s also the outward sign of a watershed moment.
  In 2019, 373,000 industrial robots were sold and put into use,  according to the International Federation of Robotics, a not-for-profit industry organization that conducts an annual, global robot census based on vendor data. That number has grown about 11% a year since 2014, to a total of 2.7 million industrial robots in use world-wide. Industrial robots—descendants of the  Unimate robot arm first installed at a  General Motors factory in  1961—are the kind common in manufacturing, performing tasks like welding, painting and assembly. They work hard, but they’re not very smart.
  Also in 2019, 173,000 “professional service robots” were sold and installed, according to the federation. That number is projected to reach 537,000 units a year—a threefold increase—by 2023. These are the kind of robots businesses use outside of manufacturing. They perform a wide variety of functions, including  defense,  warehouse automation and  disinfection in hospitals.
    These robots tend to be much smarter, equipped with advanced software, sensors and Wi-Fi or other forms of connectivity. And instead of being hidden away in factories like industrial robots, they can generally do their jobs alongside people, instead of in walled-off areas where humans are forbidden to go.
  If current growth rates for both of these kinds of robots hold, and we include professional-service robots not counted by the robotics federation, such as those made by companies solely for their own use, then some time in the coming year or so it’s likely service robots will overtake industrial robots in units sold or installed. That trend will bring new benefits for companies and consumers—and new challenges for workers.
  By far the greatest share of professional service robots are those used in logistics. Mick Mountz, who founded Kiva Systems in 2003, helped pioneer the use of software, connectivity and sensors—coupled with off-the-shelf parts like motors, gearboxes, batteries and tires—to create relatively affordable robots that were more flexible and adaptable than their forebears, the industrial robots.
  “The main difference between automation today and what we had 50 or 60 years ago is that we added software,” says Mr. Mountz. Just as critical was wireless connectivity—Wi-Fi was new at the time—and off-the-shelf sensors, like the black-and-white cameras used in the original Kiva robots, he adds.
  Amazon bought Kiva in 2012, and it became Amazon Robotics. Kiva’s robots and software remain the foundation for perhaps the best-known example of service robots today: The rolling,  pizza box-shaped “drive units” used by Amazon in its warehouses to move shelves of goods to the humans who pick and pack customers’ orders.
    Many areas of our industrial world, from transportation to manufacturing, have yet to be affected by the newer kind of automation and robotics, but probably will be, adds Mr. Mountz.
  The new generation of robots has already proved adaptable to an astonishing array of tasks, as evident in National Science Foundation-funded research conducted by  Robin Murphy, director of the Humanitarian Robotics and AI Laboratory at Texas A&M University. Early in the course of the global pandemic, Dr. Murphy and her team set out to study the ways robots were being used to help humans adapt to the effects of Covid-19. She and her team  documented 326 different robots,  used in 29 different applications, telemedicine and hospital disinfection to quarantine enforcement, delivery, telepresence, construction, agriculture, logistics and laboratory automation.
  Of these, 87% were existing robots adapted to help cope with the new virus, says Dr. Murphy. The sheer number and variety of mature robot technologies available for use in fighting the pandemic showed how companies and organizations are now spoiled for robot choice, she adds.
  Oregon State’s Dr. Knight says the pandemic likely accelerated adoption of robots. While humans are generally averse to change, wars and natural disasters can inspire very rapid shifts. “There are different styles of innovation, but responding to necessity is one of the most impactful ones,” she says.
  All the things that are not counted as robots by analysts and data-gatherers illustrate just how broad robotics has become, and how autonomy is now a thing that everyday objects can be imbued with. A full accounting could plausibly include all the autonomous drones recently authorized to fly in the U.S.  by the Federal Aviation Administration, the many autonomous vehicles made by Waymo and its competitors, the rapidly growing population of smaller  wheeled delivery robots,  ocean-crossing autonomous ships, tens of millions of  robot vacuum cleaners, a half-century of  unmanned spacecraft, and, as they become ever more connected and “smarter,” perhaps even our homes.
    In 2019, the world’s first “robot hotel” was  forced to eliminate more than half of its 243 robots, because they made life more difficult for guests and co-workers. In November 2020,  Walmart  scrapped its plans to have robots take inventory of its stores’ shelves, after discovering that humans could do the job more efficiently. Many more tasks for which robots seem well-suited have proved to be beyond their  still quite-limited cognitive abilities, forcing companies to hire humans to  pilot them remotely.
    Despite these  teething problems, we are facing a likely acceleration in the pace at which robots and automation challenge American workers, says Mark Muro, a senior fellow at the Brookings Institution. Automation has always been most disruptive to the lives of blue-collar workers. But as robots transition from displacing people on the factory floor to displacing them in the service industry, which in the U.S. is many times larger than manufacturing, it could affect the lives of millions more people.
  According to a historical analysis conducted by Mr. Muro and others at Brookings, recessions such as the one we’ve been in are the times businesses are  most likely to replace humans with automation. This happens because, when recessions hit, revenue falls faster than wages. The result is that automation goes from a nice-to-have to a perceived necessity for cash-strapped companies.
  Even when the economy recovers, that automation  isn’t going away, adds Mr. Muro. While in the long run automation increases economic productivity and  creates more jobs, in the short term it can mean unemployment and worse jobs for those swept aside by it. There is also the chronic and worsening problem that America is experiencing  ever-greater economic inequality despite increased productivity from automation.
    When she gave talks a decade ago, says Dr. Knight, she told her audiences that the robot revolution was already well under way, only it was happening behind closed doors, in places like factories and warehouses. What’s different now is that the robot revolution is happening in public, and is therefore unavoidable, even personal. In our homes, our places of work, on our streets, in our skies, robots are becoming a part of our everyday lives as they have never before.
  A century after Čapek introduced the word robot to the English language, the one thing real-life robots have yet to do is run amok and destroy us all, as they did in his play and in countless works of science fiction since. But there’s one thing he did get right: As their ranks swell, and as they take on more tasks in more places, robots are, in their own way, taking over.
  Appeared in the January 23, 2021, print edition as 'The Reality of Robots Sprints Past Fiction.'
title: 回顾捷克戏剧“ R.U.R.”一个世纪之后的工业和服务机器人技术的现状。 在英语中引入了“机器人”一词
title_en: A look at the current state of industrial and service robotics, a century after the Czech play “R.U.R.” introduced the word “robot” to the English language

note:
据《华尔街日报》报道，杰夫·贝佐斯（Jeff Bezos）（民主党的坚决支持者）和亚马逊正计划推迟在阿拉巴马州的一个仓库举行工会投票。有趣的是，亚马逊已要求美国国家劳动关系委员会重新考虑允许邮寄投票，并声称邮寄投票程序存在“严重的系统性缺陷”。
 知道了？贝索斯和亚马逊正在竭尽全力防止工人为工会投下选票时的任何阴暗活动，他们特别关注邮寄选票的完整性。
 您可能想弄清楚这是怎么累的，对不对？贝佐斯（Bezos）拥有的《华盛顿邮报》（Washington Post）称，唐纳德·特朗普及其支持者对邮寄选民欺诈的任何主张都是危险且不可原谅的。亚马逊甚至禁止Parler在其服务器上使用，部分原因是为了确保没有人可以声称11月份的邮寄投票中发生了选民欺诈。
 但是，请不要试图使其有意义。它不应该加起来。
 在这种情况下，邮寄投票对贝索斯及其电子商务巨头来说是不利的。因此，他们现在认为邮寄投票存在严重缺陷。
 亚马逊发言人告诉美国有线电视新闻网，该公司正在寻求“有效，公正和成功的选举”，并建议只有亲自投票才能确保这一点。嗯，有趣的观点。
  到目前为止，亚马逊已经抵制了美国的工会。如果多数选票投票赞成工会成立，则每小时工作的亚马逊工人将向L先生派遣L票到Bezos的办公室。 
Bamazonunion.com解释说，亚马逊的一个工会将“给予我们就我们的工作条件进行集体谈判的权利，包括安全标准，培训，休息，薪水，福利以及其他一些使我们的工作场所变得更好的重要问题之类的东西。”
 NLRB表示：“邮件投票选举将授予因健康原因或COVID测试阳性而无法进入投票地点的员工资格。”但是，由于担心结果不利，亚马逊现在坚持认为当前的COVID爆发还没有足够危险，因此无法做出NLRB决定。
 亚马逊表示，NLRB代理区域总监Lisa Henderson“得出了一个非同寻常的结论，即员工中任何水平的感染或潜在感染都被视为爆发。”在截至1月7日的14天期间，其Bessemer工厂仅有218名员工，占2.88％，测试结果呈阳性，亚马逊显然不认为这是“爆发”。这是观点的转变。想象一下，如果在11月之前有人问过类似的问题，《华盛顿邮报》的报道。
 邮寄投票从来没有关系到美国人的健康或确保每个声音的重要性。一如既往，我们国家在华盛顿和硅谷最有影响力的人物，其意见仅基于增加其自身力量，财务和灵活性的成果。
note_en:
Jeff Bezos — a strong Democratic supporter — and Amazon are aiming to postpone a unionization vote at one of its warehouses in Alabama, the  Wall Street Journal  reports. Interestingly, Amazon has requested that the National Labor Relations Board reconsider allowing mail-in voting, claiming that the mail-in voting process has “serious and systemic flaws.”
 Got that? Bezos and Amazon are doing all they can to prevent any shady activity when workers cast their ballots for unionization, and they are particularly concerned about the integrity of mail-in ballots.
 You are likely trying to figure out how that adds up, right? The  Washington Post, owned by Bezos, called any claims of mail-in voter fraud by Donald Trump and his supporters dangerous and inexcusable. Amazon even banned Parler from its servers, in part to ensure no one could claim voter fraud occurred from mail-in ballots back in November.
 But stop trying to make it make sense. It is not supposed to add up.
 In this case, mail-in voting is disadvantageous for Bezos and his e-commerce behemoth. Therefore, they now consider mail-in voting seriously flawed.
 An Amazon spokesperson  tells CNN the company is seeking a “valid, fair and successful election” and suggests that only in-person voting can ensure that. Hmm, interesting perspective.
  Amazon has, thus far, fended off unions in the United States. If a majority of ballots vote in favor of unionization, hourly Amazon workers would send an L right up to Bezos’ office.
 Bamazonunion.com explains that a union at Amazon would “give us the right to collectively bargain over our working conditions including items such as safety standards, training, breaks, pay, benefits, and other important issues that would make our workplace better.”
 The NLRB says, “A mail ballot election will enfranchise employees who cannot enter the voting location for health reasons or due to positive COVID tests.” Fearing an unfavorable outcome, however, Amazon now insists that current COVID outbreaks aren’t dangerous enough to warrant this NLRB decision.
 Amazon says that Lisa Henderson, NLRB’s Acting Regional Director “reached the remarkable conclusion that any level of infection or potential infection among employees counts as an ‘outbreak.'”  O nly 218 people employees, 2.88%, at its Bessemer facility tested positive during the 14-day period ending on January 7, which Amazon does not consider an “outbreak” apparently. This is quite the change of perspective. Imagine the coverage from the  Washington Post if similar questions had been asked before November.
 Mail-in voting has never been about the health of Americans or ensuring that each voice counts. As always, our country’s most powerful figures in both Washington and Silicon Valley base their opinions solely on the outcomes that increase their own power, finances, and flexibility.
title: 杰夫·贝佐斯（Jeff Bezos）和亚马逊（Amazon）不想让他们的工人对工会进行邮件投票
title_en: Jeff Bezos and Amazon do not want their workers voting by mail on unionization

视频显示了最新的Flipper Zero示例。研磨外壳，即在CNC上从一块塑料上切下然后涂漆。最终版本将注塑成型，并在塑料颗粒内部预混颜色。但是最终的设备将看起来完全像这样。由于内部增加了两个PCB，因此Flipper的尺寸有所增加。
 接下来，我们将讨论当前项目状态，任务和问题，即将到来的制造路线图以及对质押经理的访问。
  我们将诚实地说：我们将无法在2月份将Flippers运送给所有支持者。这些设备将逐步生产，从非常小的批次开始，然后逐渐增加体积，以减少出现任何缺陷的风险。我们真的想制造出一个在机械到电子各方面都完美的完美产品。大批量中的任何错误都是致命的，将无法撤回。
 鉴于我们支持者的巨大期望，我们根本无法发布未完成的设备。我们将逐步生产小批量产品并将其运送给友好的开发人员以及那些采用早期采用者计划的人员。这些人将充当Beta测试人员，将安装新的固件版本并填写错误报告。但是在申请早期采用者之前，请记住，您最终可能会遇到带有严重错误的设备。
  我们希望第一批小批量产品在机械和电子学方面存在缺陷，经过一段时间使用后，这些缺陷将变得明显。例如，使用一个月后，操纵杆上的按钮可能偶尔卡住，USB端口或iButton弹簧针可能会断开。我们当然会运行自动测试，但是它们并不能涵盖可能出现的所有潜在问题。因此，我们将此类批次标记为高风险。到春季末，我们希望能够稳定生产经过全面测试的设备。
  如果您愿意冒险尽早获得Flipper，请注册“早期采用者计划”。您应该意识到，您可能会遇到机械缺陷和其他问题。您可以在此处申请（仅适用于Kickstarter支持者）。
 请记住，固件现在非常不稳定：它有很多错误，许多功能尚未完成，某些功能的图形界面尚未准备好，必须从控制台启动而不显示它们他们在屏幕上。每天我们都会发布新的固件版本，所有Beta测试人员都必须不断更新和测试它们。 
当有足够的开发人员手握Flippers来贡献固件代码时，Github上的固件存储库将打开。我们计划在Flipper发行后至少两年内对固件进行深入支持，因此我们会提前准备好所有支持者，以便在您收到设备时，并非所有功能都可以在那使用。这些功能将随着时间的推移在固件更新中添加。
  在几天之内，我们将打开对质押管理器的访问权限，您将可以在其中指定送货地址并付款，选择箱体颜色，并在现有订单中添加更多的脚蹼。也可以在订单中添加硅胶套和特殊的开发模块。现在，我们已经完成了运输服务API的集成，可以根据包裹的重量自动计算税款和运输成本。
  有权访问质押管理器的邀请将发送到您在Kickstarter个人资料上设置的电子邮件地址以及Kickstarter收件箱中。不要害怕错过这封信，在您完成所有步骤之前，系统会不断提醒您。那些在一个月内未完成调查的支持者将另行通知。
  硬件开发是我们的首要任务，因为与固件不同，硬件一旦发布并交付给用户就无法升级。这里的任何缺陷都是致命的。
  现在，我们正在完成天线的微调，调整组件值并消除较小的粗糙度。仍然需要在极端条件下（例如温度下降，振动，冲击等）测试Flipper。一般来说，可以说硬件设计已经完成。现在，我们仅根据认证实验室和工厂的要求进行较小的更正。
  对于我们来说，这是最具挑战性的子系统。仅在单个收发器上支持300-900 MHz范围是一项非常困难的任务。我们使用的电路具有三个（！）切换无线电路径和我们自己的天线设计。这种电路的复杂性在于它必须通过所有认证标准（FCC，EC和其他认证标准），例如寄生谐波，频率偏差等。
 现在，我们正在根据认证实验室的规则和标准调整天线馈电系统的特性。这是一个耗时的过程。 
为了将设备正式进口到欧盟和美国并在亚马逊等商店出售，我们需要在这些国家/地区获得RF符合性证书。事实是，对于不同的国家，这些标准是不同的，我们需要制造与不同标准相对应的几种不同版本的设备。这些是远离开发和生产的法律任务，但是它们需要时间，我们无法避免。它们还使后勤工作变得复杂，因为我们必须为每个地区制造具有不同SKU的几种不同设备版本。 （区域差异仅在固件级别）
   车身成型和组装的准备工作包括数十个非常小的修复程序。现在，我们正在与工厂协调所有生产细节。必须考虑如何将零件从模具中取出，塑料注入点将在哪里以及每种零件的哪种成型方法是最佳的。
  工厂进行调整，计算材料应力，冷却速率，流道位置等。此过程的困难在于，任何机械重新设计都涉及修改内部电子设备的位置并影响组装过程。因此，即使加强板移动了0.1mm，也经常会导致PCB外形的变化。这是一个耗时的过程，因为它需要所有团队一次参与。
  三个工厂参与生产，每个工厂都有自己的要求。现在，我们正在每个阶段设置组装和测试流程。
  整个组装过程有严格的时间要求，每个步骤，每个零件和测试过程都应符合计划的时间间隔。如果一步需要更长的时间，将不可能以可控的方式生产成千上万的设备。目前，组装一个脚蹼以及进行测试和固件更新的时间不得超过420秒！
 我们准备过程和设备的预组装零件以适应此时的情况。这就是汇编说明。
  首先，有一个单独模块的中间组件，例如将一个红外窗口焊接到顶箱盖上。为此，将塑料针与特殊的烙铁融化。 
弹簧和操纵杆按钮安装在屏幕覆盖物中。该元素由8个部分组成：按扣，3个弹簧，3个按钮（中心，大圆圈，后退按钮），状态LED导光板。
  主PCB通过操纵杆和按钮连接至屏幕覆盖层，并闩锁至PCB，以形成可组装的模块。
       总的来说，组装包括20个步骤，最后以安装底盖为结束。然后，将设备发送到固件刷新和测试。
  Flipper组成的大多数零件都是从供应商处订购的串行组件。但是，有一些专门为Flipper定制的电子组件。这就要求与实体制造商下订单生产每个组件，并就细节和协议进行沟通。
  我们需要的天线不能简单地购买，因此我们根据图纸特别订购该线形天线的绕组。它应能同时在300至900 MHz的所有频率范围内正常工作，并同时适合我们的情况。现在，我们正在检查制造的天线是否确实符合我们的要求。
   对于iButton垫，我们使用非标准长度的引脚，因此我们必须单独订购其生产。
   该组件也是非标准的，是为我们生产的。对于原型，我们结合使用了两个带有8针和2针的接头，这不适合最终器件。要找到好的割台制造商并不容易。 
我们非常担心最终发货日期的延迟，但是希望您能理解。 对于我们许多人来说，这是我们一生中最重要的项目，因此我们尽力满足您的最高期望。 现在，释放劣质产品并使您失望的恐惧是我们的主要动力。
note_en:
The video shows the latest Flipper Zero sample. The case is milled, that is, cut from a piece of plastic on CNC and then painted. The final version will be injection molded with color premixed inside the plastic grains. But the final device will look exactly like this. The Flipper has gotten slightly bigger due to two additional PCBs inside.
 Next, we'll talk about the current project status, tasks and problems, upcoming manufacturing roadmap and access to the pledge manager.
  We'll be honest:  we won't be able to ship Flippers to all backers in February. The devices will be produced gradually, starting with a very small batches and gradually increasing in volume to reduce the risk of having any defects. We really want to make a cool product that is perfect in every aspect from mechanics to electronics. Any mistake in a large batch will be fatal and it will be impossible to withdraw.
 Given the huge expectations of our backers, we simply cannot afford to release an unfinished device. We will gradually produce and ship small batches to friendly developers and those who applied to the early adopters program. These people will act as beta testers, will install new firmware versions and fill the bug reports. But before you apply into an early adopters, keep in mind that you might end up with a device that has some serious bugs.
  We expect the first small batch to contain defects in mechanics and electronics, which will become apparent after a period of use. For example, after a month of usage, buttons on the joystick may stuck occasionally, a USB port or, for example, iButton pogo-pins could break out. We will of course run automated tests, but they do not cover all potential problems that may arise. Therefore, we mark such batches as high risk. By the end of spring, we expect to have a stable mass production of fully tested devices.
  If you're willing to take the risk to get Flipper early, sign up for the Early Adopters Program. You should be aware that you may face mechanical defects and other problems. You can apply  here  (only available to Kickstarter backers).
 Keep in mind that the firmware is now very unstable at the moment: it has a lot of bugs, many features are yet to be completed, the graphical interface for some features is not ready, and they have to be launched from the console without displaying them on the screen. Every day we release a new firmware builds and all beta testers will have to constantly update and test them.
  The firmware repository on Github will be open by the time there are enough developers with Flippers on hand to contribute to the firmware code. We plan to intensively support the firmware for at least 2 years after the release of Flipper, so we prepare all backers in advance that when you receive the device, not all features may work there. These features will be added over time in firmware updates.
  Within a few days, we will open access to the pledge manager, where you'll be able to specify the shipping address and pay for delivery, select the case color and add more Flippers to an existing order. It will also be possible to add a silicone case and special development modules to the order. Now we are finishing the shipping service API integration to automatically calculate taxes and shipping costs depending on the weight of the parcel.
  An invite with access to the pledge manager will be sent to your email address, which you set on the Kickstarter profile, and to your Kickstarter inbox. Do not be afraid to miss the letter, the system will keep reminding you about it until you complete all the steps. Those backers who don't complete the survey within a month will be notified separately.
  Hardware development was our top priority because hardware, unlike firmware, cannot be upgraded once released and shipped to the users. Any flaw here would be fatal.
  Now we are finishing the antennas fine-tuning, tweaking the component values and eliminating minor roughness. It remains to test Flipper with extreme conditions like temperature drops, vibration, shock, and more. In general, we can say that the hardware design is complete. Now we only make minor corrections that are requested by the certifying laboratory and the factory.
  This is the most challenging subsystem for us. The 300-900 MHz range support just on a single transceiver is a very difficult task. We use a circuit with three (!) switching radio paths and our own antenna design. The complexity of such a circuit is that it must pass all certification standards (FCC, EC and others), such as parasitic harmonics, frequency deviations, etc.
 Now we are adjusting the characteristics of the antenna feed system to the rules and standards of the certification laboratory. This is a time-consuming process.
   In order to officially import the device into the EU and the US and sell it in stores like Amazon, we need to obtain a certificate of RF compliance in those countries. The fact is that for different countries these standards are different, and we need to make several different versions of devices that correspond to different standards. These are legal tasks that are far from development and production, but they take time and we cannot avoid them. They also complicate logistics as we must make several different device versions with different SKUs for each region.  (Regional differences will only be at the firmware level)
   The preparation for body molding and assembly consists of dozens of very small fixes. Now we are coordinating with the factory on all production details. It is necessary to take into account how the part will be removed from the mold, where the plastic injection points will be and what type of molding is optimal for each part.
  The factory makes adjustments, calculates material stress, cooling rates, runner positions, etc. The difficulty with this process is that any mechanics redesign involves revising the position of the electronics inside and affects the assembly process. Therefore, even a 0.1mm shift of the stiffeners can often lead to changes in the PCB outline. This is a time-consuming process as it requires the participation of all teams at once.
  Three factories are involved in production, and each of them sets its own requirements. Now we are setting up the assembly and testing flow at every stage.
  The entire assembly process has a strict time requirements, each step, each part and test process should fit into scheduled time interval. Should one step take longer, it won't be possible to produce tens of thousands of devices in a controllable way. Currently the assembly of one Flipper along with testing and firmware flashing must take no longer than 420 seconds!
 We prepare the process and pre-assembled parts of the device to fit in this time. This is what the assembly instructions look like.
  First, there is an intermediate assembly of individual modules, for example an infrared window is soldered to the top case cover. To do this, plastic pin is melted with a special soldering iron.
  The springs and joystick buttons are installed in the screen overlay. This element consists of 8 parts: a snap, 3 springs, 3 buttons (center, big circle, back button), status LED light guide.
  The main PCB connects to the screen overlay with joystick and button, and latches to the PCBs to form a ready for assembly module.
       In total, the assembly consists of 20 steps, which ends with the installation of the bottom cover. Then the device is sent for firmware flashing and testing.
  Most of the parts which Flipper consists of are serial components ordered from suppliers. But there are a few electronic components that are custom made specifically for Flipper. This requires placing an order for the production of each component with a solid manufacturer, communicate on details and agreements.
  The antenna we need cannot simply be bought, so we order the winding of this wire antenna specially according to our drawings. It should simultaneously work well on all the ranges from 300 to 900 MHz and at the same time fit into our case. Now we are checking that the manufactured antennas really meet our requirements.
   For the iButton pad, we use a pin of a non-standard length, so we had to order its production separately.
   This component is also non-standard and is produced for us. For prototypes, we used a combination of two headers with 8 pins and 2 pins, which is not suitable for the final device. Finding a manufacturer of good headers was not easy.
   We are very concerned about final shipping date postponement, but we hope you can understand it. This is the most important project of our entire life for many of us, that's why we try our best to meet your highest expectations. The fear of releasing bad product and disappointing you is our main motivation now.
title: 鳍零生产和运输计划
title_en: Flipper Zero Manufacturing and Shipping Plan

note:
阿里巴巴是寻找世界上最酷，有时甚至最怪异的电动汽车的最佳场所之一。作为本周令人震惊的阿里巴巴电动车新系列的一部分，我们来看看一些我们的最爱。
 本周的功能是一款专为公用事业和越野用途而设计的功能强大的小型电动皮卡车，尽管它在美国甚至可能是街头合法的新能源汽车。
  如果这辆电动皮卡车的比例看起来有些奇怪，那是因为它们是正确的。
  我们正在谈论的是12英寸的车轮，一个亲密的两人驾驶室以及在1.6 m（5’3“）长的床上可承载500 kg（1,100 lb）的负载。
 但是，尽管它可能很小，但它仍然是功能齐全的卡车。床不仅有后挡板，而且侧面也向下折叠以变成平床。驾驶室配备了您期望的所有基本汽车装备，例如收音机，通风孔（空调额外收费），雨刷，可调节座椅，手动锁/窗户和三点式安全带，以确保安全。前面甚至还有64升（2.3立方英尺）的垃圾！
 这不仅是美化的高尔夫球车，还是一种小型但设备齐全的多功能车。
  我与公司的Reyna Zoe聊天，后者与我分享了更详细的规格表。 
她能为我提供的最佳价格是标准版3950美元，扩展版驾驶室4880美元，配有四个座椅，更大的13英寸轮毂和其他一些升级装置，包括动力转向，电子锁/窗户，甚至还有儿童安全锁。
 尽管她很乐于助人，但我一直在等待几天才能到达我在美国最近的港口的运输报价（雷娜向我解释说，不幸的是，他们不提供门到门运输作为选择）。我猜我们将要收取大约1,000至1,500美元的运费，尽管费用可能会有很大差异。
 总而言之，即使总价格突破了5,000美元，这对于一辆看起来不错的小型多功能卡车来说也不错。而且，如果您考虑每天为使用多功能车的农民或建筑工人增加多少汽油和柴油成本，那么这个价格实际上是合理的。
 甚至对于我们其他人来说，它看起来也像是有趣的小逃生。不相信我吗？在下面查看卖家的视频，并尝试告诉我这看起来并不有趣！
  请记住，尽管这是NEV（邻居电动车），因此其最高时速仅为40 km / h（25 mph）。提供后轮驱动的4 kW电动机的档位较低，扭矩较小，而不是转速较高。
 这不是要在高速公路上行驶，而是要完成当地任务或在工作现场附近行驶。您不会在高尔夫球车中拖拉十几袋水泥，但是您可以在这小东西中。
 它的72V和7.2 kWh电池组甚至可以在120公里（75英里）处达到合适的续航里程。 
您可能已经猜到了，卡车使用的是老式铅酸电池。尽管锂离子电池也可以提高价格，但仅减轻锂离子电池的重量就可能会大大增加续航里程。
 如果您对卡车熟悉，那么您可能会认为它是Pickman，这是我们之前介绍过的中国电动迷你卡车。
 我不确定此清单是否与在阿里巴巴上转售的卡车相同，或者它们是否只是根据关于Pickman照片的版权法而游刃有余。
 如果是这样，这将是让自己成为Pickman电动卡车的便宜得多的方式。它们实际上目前在美国销售，但基本型号的起价为1万美元。它们甚至在公开道路上均以低速车辆（LSV）的形式在最高时速达35 mph的街道上合法行驶。
 CGTN在洛杉矶的Pickman卡车上做了一块，您可以在下面看到。
  如您所见，这辆小卡车尽管身材矮小，但实际上似乎还不错。
 虽然这并不能代替Cyber​​truck或电动F-150，但它可能是一款有趣的小型多功能电动汽车，适用于不需要全尺寸EV卡车的功率，速度和有效载荷能力的工作场所和运输工作。 
我有点像阿里巴巴的迷。我已经在该平台上进行了数十次购买，更不用说在以零售为重点的AliExpress平台上进行了数百次购买。
 以奇异，有趣和超值的价格找到怪异，有趣和超赞的产品的好地方。
 为了减轻您的担心，他们将您的资金保留在托管帐户中，并提供交易保证，以使交互更安全。我很少遇到订单问题。几次遇到问题，我几乎总是拿回了我全部或大部分的钱。
 但是，这些都不应该被视为对阿里巴巴或本产品本身的认可。像这样在阿里巴巴橱窗购物并找到最有趣的利基电动汽车很有趣，但订购过程需要技巧，耐心和勇气的结合。您必须非常清楚地传达您想要的信息，以确保您和卖方在同一页面上，并且您能收到期望的信息。
 即使您已经与卖方商定了上门付款的运费，在产品到达您时，也不可避免地会产生大量费用。
 在阿里巴巴购物，特别是针对大笔购买，并不是为了胆小。但是，当您以最低价找到有趣的产品时，这可能会非常有意义。
  您正在阅读Electrek，这是专家，他们日复一日地发布有关特斯拉，电动汽车和绿色能源的新闻。请务必查看我们的主页以获取所有最新新闻，并在Twitter，Facebook和LinkedIn上关注Electrek以保持关注。不知道从哪里开始？查看我们的YouTube频道以获取最新评论。
note_en:
Alibaba is one of the best places around to find the coolest and sometimes weirdest electric vehicles in the world. As part of a new series known as  Awesomely weird Alibaba electric vehicle of the week, we’re taking a look at some of our favorites.
 This week’s feature is  a small-yet-mighty electric pickup truck designed for utility and off-road usage, though it may even be street legal as an NEV in the US.
  If the proportions look at bit odd on this electric pickup truck, that’s because they are.
  We’re talking 12-inch wheels, an intimate two person cab and a payload capacity of 500 kg (1,100 lb) in the 1.6 m (5’3″) long bed .
 But while it may be small, this is still a fully functional truck. The bed doesn’t just have a tailgate, the sides fold down as well to transform into a flat bed. The cab has all the basic automotive accoutrements you’d expect, such as a radio, air vents (A/C costs extra), windshield wipers, adjustable seats, manual locks/windows and three-point seat belts for safety. There’s even a 64L (2.3 cubic feet) frunk up front!
 This isn’t just a glorified golf cart, it’s a small yet well-equipped utility vehicle.
  I chatted with Reyna Zoe from the company, who shared with me the  more detailed spec sheet.
 The best price she could offer me was  US$3,950 for the standard model, or  US$4,880 for the extended cab model that comes with four seats, larger 13-inch wheels and a few other upgrades including power steering, electric locks/windows and even a child safety lock.
 As helpful as she is, I’ve been waiting days for a shipping quote to my nearest port in the US (Reyna explained to me that they unfortunately don’t offer door-to-door shipping as an option). I’m guessing we’re going to be looking at somewhere around US$1,000 to US$1,500 in shipping charges, though these can vary wildly.
 All told, even if the total price breaks US$5,000, that’s not bad for what looks like a nice little utility truck. And if you think about how much the cost of gas and diesel adds up for farmers or construction workers who use utility vehicles every day, this price might actually make sense.
 And even for the rest of us, it looks like fun little runabout. Don’t believe me? Check out the seller’s video below and try to tell me that doesn’t look like fun!
  Keep in mind though that this is an NEV (Neighborhood Electric Vehicle) and thus has a max speed of just 40 km/h (25 mph). The 4 kW motor providing rear-wheel drive is geared low for torque, not speed.
 This isn’t for cruising the highway, it’s for getting local errands done or cruising around a job site. You aren’t going to be hauling a dozen bags of cement in a golf cart, but you could in this little thing.
 And it even has decent range at 120 km (75 miles) from its 72V and 7.2 kWh battery pack.
  As you might have guessed, the truck uses old-fashioned lead acid batteries. The weight savings alone from li-ion batteries would likely boost the range significantly, though they’d also boost the price as well.
 If the truck looks familiar to you, then you might be recognizing it as the Pickman, a  Chinese electric mini-truck we’ve covered before.
 I’m not sure if this listing is the same truck being resold on Alibaba or if they are just playing fast and loose with copyright laws regarding the Pickman’s photos.
 If it is, this would be a much cheaper way to get yourself a Pickman electric truck. They are actually  currently being sold in the US, but start at $10k for the base model. They are even street legal as Low Speed Vehicles (LSVs) on public roads posted up to 35 mph.
 CGTN did a piece on the Pickman truck in LA, which you can see below.
  As you can see, the little truck actually seems to be decently capable despite its small stature.
 While this isn’t going to replace  a Cybertruck or electric F-150, it could be an interesting little utility EV for work sites and transport jobs that don’t need the power, speed and payload capacity of a full-size EV truck.
   I’m something of an Alibaba junkie. I’ve made a few dozen purchases on the platform, not to mention several hundred purchases on the more retail-focused AliExpress platform.
 It’s a great to place to find weird, interesting and awesome products at weird, interesting and awesome prices.
 To ease your worried mind, they hold your money in escrow and offer trade assurance to make interactions safer. I’ve rarely had an issue with an order. The few times I’ve had problems, I’ve almost always gotten all or most of my money back.
 However, none of this should be taken as an endorsement of Alibaba or this product itself. While it’s fun to window shop through Alibaba like this and find the most interesting niche electric vehicles out there, the ordering process requires a combination of finesse, patience and courage. You have to be very clear in communicating what you want to ensure that you and the seller are on the same page and that you receive what you’re expecting.
 There’s also inevitably going to be a number of fees thrown on top by the time the product reaches you, even if you’ve agreed with the seller on door to door shipping paid upfront.
 Shopping on Alibaba, especially for large purchases, is not for the faint of heart. But it can be quite rewarding when you find interesting products at rock bottom prices.
  You’re reading Electrek— experts who break news about  Tesla,  electric vehicles, and  green energy, day after day. Be sure to check out our  homepage for all the latest news, and follow Electrek on  Twitter,  Facebook, and  LinkedIn to stay in the loop. Don’t know where to start? Check out our  YouTube channel for the latest reviews.

凶狼是标志性的野兽。这些绝种的更新世食肉动物已经从洛杉矶的La Brea Tar Pits那里发现。借助电视连续剧《权力的游戏》，庞大的犬科动物甚至已经成为人们关注的焦点。但是，一项关于可怕的狼遗传学的新研究使古生物学家大为震惊：它发现这些动物根本不是狼，而是北美进化出的最后一个犬系。
 自从1850年代首次描述狼以来，可怕的狼就吸引了现代人类的想象力。从爱达荷州到玻利维亚，在美洲大部分地区都发现了他们的遗体。著名的La Brea沥青渗入物证明了被困在焦油中的猎物如何诱使许多这些冰河时代的捕食者致死。凶残的狼用焦油保存的遗骸揭示了一个气势磅的猎人，长达六英尺，头骨和下巴的适应能力可以摧毁庞大而艰难的大型动物。尽管这些犬科动物已经明显进化为可以处理野牛，马，野牛和其他大型食草动物，然后在美洲漫游，但是在可怕的狼和当今较小的灰狼之间的骨骼相似性表明它们是近亲。长期以来，人们一直认为，北美大灰狼在北美以狼为生，然后灰狼从欧亚大陆穿过白令陆桥。现在，一些保存完好的DNA似乎从根本上改变了故事。
 这项新研究于周三在《自然》杂志上发表，最初是为了了解可怕狼的生物学基础。 “对我而言，这是从一项决定出发，到美国各地巡回收集狼的标本，然后看看我们能得到什么，因为当时还没有人设法从狼的标本中提取DNA，”达勒姆大学考古学家说，研究合著者安吉拉·佩里（Angela Perri）。同时，澳大利亚阿德莱德大学的遗传学家和合著者基兰·米切尔（Kieren Mitchell）也在试图从可怕的狼残骸中提取和研究古代DNA －最终与该项目合作的其他实验室也是如此。
 研究人员提出的问题之一是可怕的狼与其他狼之间的关系。几十年来，古生物学家一直在评论恶狼和灰狼的骨骼有多相似。有时很难区分它们。米切尔说：“我的直觉是，凶猛的狼可能是灰太狼的专门血统或亚种。”
 但是新的证据告诉了一个不同的故事。初步的遗传分析表明，可怕的狼和灰狼不是近亲。 “当我说结果绝对令人惊讶时，我认为我可以代表整个团队，” Perri说。
 在对50,000至13,000年间的可怕的狼化石中的五个基因组进行测序后，研究人员发现这些动物属于狗的古老系。现在看来，恶狼已经在美洲进化，并且与欧亚大陆的灰狼没有近亲。灰狼和可怕的狼最后一次共享祖先是在570万年前。研究人员说，两者之间的强烈相似之处是融合进化的情况，不同的物种由于相似的生活方式而具有相似的适应性甚至外观。有时这样的会合只是粗糙的，例如鸟类和蝙蝠尽管翅膀在结构上有所不同，但它们都在进化。对于凶猛的灰狼，追逐大型食草动物在蹄上抓肉的生活导致了两个不同的犬科血统，独立地产生了狼状形态。
 未参与这项新研究的育空古生物学家格兰特·扎祖拉（Yukon古生物学家）格兰特·扎祖拉（Grant Zazula）说：“这些结果彻底颠覆了可怕的狼只是灰狼的大表亲的想法。实际上，两者之间的相似性使得灰狼被视为恶狼的生物学和行为的代理，从from群动力学到动物the叫声。可怕的狼的新身份意味着，许多先前的假设（直到生活中的样子）都需要重新调查。扎祖拉说：“对化石骨骼中古代DNA和蛋白质的研究正在迅速改写冰河时期以及北美哺乳动物的最新历史。” 
用技术术语来说，新发现意味着可怕的狼可能需要一个新的属名来表明它们不再属于灰狼所属的犬属。 Perri，Mitchell及其同事建议Aenocyon，意思是“可怕的狼”。但是研究人员并不希望他们的发现会完全颠覆传统，而Aenocyon dirus很可能会继续被称为“恶狼”。佩里说：“他们只会加入诸如狼之类的杂物俱乐部，但并非如此。”
 新的发现也增加了专家对为什么最后一个冰河时代结束后凶猛的狼最终消失的反省。这些掠食者在数百万年的时间里专门从事狩猎骆驼，马，野牛和其他食草动物。这些猎物的来源消失了，可怕的狼也消失了。佩里说：“与灰狼相比，灰狼是适应的模型，而灰狼似乎在应对不断变化的环境和猎物时灵活性较差。”
 可怕的狼也没有在其远古的骨头中留下正在腐烂的DNA之外的遗传遗产。尽管像狼和土狼这样的犬科动物经常会产生杂种，但可怕的狼显然没有与今天仍然存活的任何其他犬科动物杂交。 Perri，Mitchell和他们的同事没有发现可怕的狼与灰狼或土狼之间存在杂交的DNA证据。米切尔（Mitchell）指出，恶狼是与其他犬科动物遗传隔离的，因此“杂交无法提供解决方案”，因为恶狼可能无法利用最近从欧亚大陆传入的狼来繁殖后代。
 到13,000年前，可怕的狼已经灭绝。祖祖拉指出，在欧亚大陆恶劣，多变的环境中演变可能使灰狼处于优势地位，“而冰河末期，大灰狼却在加利福尼亚南部放松了警惕。”但是听起来可怕的狼故事的结局实际上仅仅是开始。保存下来的基因表明，恶狼及其祖先在美洲已经超过500万年之久了，而他们故事的早期章节正等待改写。
note_en:
Dire wolves are iconic beasts. Thousands of these extinct Pleistocene carnivores have been recovered from the La Brea Tar Pits in Los Angeles. And the massive canids have even received some time in the spotlight thanks to the television series  Game of Thrones. But a new study of dire wolf genetics has startled paleontologists: it found that these animals were not wolves at all, but rather the last of a dog lineage that evolved in North America.
 Ever since they were first described in the 1850s, dire wolves have captured modern humans’ imagination. Their remains have been found throughout much of the Americas, from Idaho to Bolivia. The La Brea asphalt seeps famously document how prey animals mired in tar lured many of these ice age predators to a sticky death. The dire wolves’ tar-preserved remains reveal an imposing hunter up to six feet long, with skull and jaw adaptations to take down enormous, struggling megafauna. Though these canids had clearly evolved to handle the mastodons, horses, bison and other large herbivores then roaming the Americas, skeletal resemblances between dire wolves and the smaller gray wolves of today suggested a close kinship. It had long been assumed that dire wolves made themselves at home in North America before gray wolves followed them across the Bering Land Bridge from Eurasia. Now some well-preserved DNA seems to be fundamentally changing the story.
 The new study, published on Wednesday in  Nature , began as an effort to understand dire wolves’ biological basics. “For me, it started with a decision to road-trip around the U.S. collecting dire wolf samples and see what we could get, since no one had managed to get DNA out of dire wolf samples at that point,” says Durham University archaeologist and study co-author Angela Perri. At the same time, geneticist and co-author Kieren Mitchell of the University of Adelaide in Australia was also trying to extract and study ancient DNA from dire wolf remains—as were other labs that eventually collaborated on the project.
 One of the researchers’ questions was how dire wolves were related to other wolves. For decades, paleontologists have remarked on how similar the bones of dire wolves and gray wolves are. Sometimes it is difficult to tell them apart. “My hunch was that dire wolves were possibly a specialized lineage or subspecies of gray wolf,” Mitchell says.
 But the new evidence told a different story. Preliminary genetic analyses indicated that dire and gray wolves were  not close relatives. “I think I can speak for the whole group when I say the results were definitely a surprise,” Perri says.
 After sequencing five genomes from dire wolf fossils between 50,000 and 13,000 years old, the researchers found that the animals belonged to a much older lineage of dogs. Dire wolves, it now appeared, had evolved in the Americas and had no close kinship with the gray wolves from Eurasia; the last time gray wolves and dire wolves shared a common ancestor was about 5.7 million years ago. The strong resemblance between the two, the researchers say, is a case of convergent evolution, whereby different species develop similar adaptations—or even appearances—thanks to a similar way of life. Sometimes such convergence is only rough, such as both birds and bats evolving wings despite their differing anatomy. In the case of dire and gray wolves, lives of chasing large herbivores to catch some meat on the hoof resulted in two different canid lineages independently producing wolflike forms.
 “These results totally shake up the idea that dire wolves were just bigger cousins of gray wolves,” says Yukon paleontologist Grant Zazula, who was not involved in the new study. In fact, the similarity between the two has led gray wolves to be taken as proxies for dire wolf biology and behavior, from  pack dynamics to  the sound of the animal’s howls. The dire wolf’s new identity means that many previous assumptions—down to what it looked like in life—require reinvestigation. “The study of ancient DNA and proteins from fossil bones is rapidly rewriting the ice age and more recent history of North America’s mammals,” Zazula says.
 In technical terms, the new findings mean dire wolves may need a new genus name to indicate they are no longer be part of the genus  Canis , to which gray wolves belong. Perri, Mitchell and their colleagues suggest  Aenocyon , meaning “terrible wolf.” But the researchers don’t expect their findings to completely overturn tradition, and  Aenocyon dirus would likely continue to be called the dire wolf. “They will just join the club of things like maned wolves that are called wolves but aren’t really,” Perri says.
 The new findings also add layers to experts’ ruminations on why dire wolves eventually disappeared as the last ice age closed. These predators became specialized in hunting camels, horses, bison and other herbivores in North America over millions of years. As those prey sources disappeared, so did the dire wolves. “In contrast to gray wolves, which are a model for adaptation,” Perri says, “dire wolves appear to be much less flexible to deal with changing environments and prey.”
 Nor did dire wolves leave a genetic legacy beyond the decaying DNA in their ancient bones. Although canids such as wolves and coyotes often create hybrids, dire wolves apparently did not do so with any other canids that remain alive today. Perri, Mitchell and their colleagues found no DNA evidence of interbreeding between dire wolves and gray wolves or coyotes. Dire wolves were genetically isolated from other canids, Mitchell notes, so “hybridization couldn’t provide a way out” because dire wolves were probably unable to produce viable offspring with the recently arrived wolves from Eurasia.
 By 13,000 years ago, dire wolves were facing extinction. Evolving in the harsh, variable environments of Eurasia may have given gray wolves an edge, Zazula notes, “while the big, bad dire wolves got caught off guard relaxing in southern California at the end of the ice age.” But what might sound like the end of the dire wolf’s story is really only the beginning. Preserved genes have shown that dire wolves and their ancestors were top dogs in the Americas for more than five million years—and the early chapters of their story are waiting to be rewritten.
title: 可怕的狼不是真正的狼，新的遗传线索揭示了

位于美国的定制零件制造服务提供商Protolabs已收购了位于荷兰的3D Hubs，该在线平台可为工程师提供按需访问的全球约240个制造合作伙伴网络。
 收购价格为2.8亿美元，Protolabs将以1.3亿美元现金和1.5亿美元普通股支付。在完成交易后的两年内，还需再支付5,000万美元，以绩效为基础，以现金和股票平均分配。
 有趣的是，Protolabs还建立了“基于财务业绩和员工保留目标的实现”支付给3D Hubs员工的员工激励基金。
 成立于2013年的3D Hubs表示，迄今为止，该公司已通过其数字平台促进了超过600万个定制零件和产品的生产。
 该公司得到了Balderton Capital，EQT Ventures，Endeit Capital，Hearst Ventures，DOEN Participaties，Future Shape和Rockstart等公司的支持。
 早在2015年初Tech.eu入选了我们值得关注的10家欧洲3D打印初创公司名单之后，他们就对该公司进行了访问-这是一段漫长的旅程。
 Protolabs现任美洲副总裁兼总经理兼现任总裁兼首席执行官Rob Bodor说：“添加3D Hubs可以为Protolabs提供一个平台，以发展我们的服务模型，从而为我们的客户提供前所未有的制造灵活性。” 
“我们合并后的组织将为市场提供行业领先的数字制造解决方案，以满足他们从构思到原型再到最终用途零件生产的所有需求。 我们在一起可以满足整个产品生命周期中几乎所有定制制造需求。”他补充说。
note_en:
Protolabs, the US-based provider of a manufacturing service for custom parts, has  acquired The Netherlands-based  3D Hubs, an online platform that provides engineers with on-demand access to a network of approximately 240 manufacturing partners around the world.
 The purchase price is $280 million, which Protolabs is paying with $130 million in cash and $150 million in its common stock. An additional $50 million is payable subject to performance-based targets over two years after close, split evenly in cash and stock.
 Interestingly, Protolabs has also established an employee incentive fund payable to 3D Hubs employees “based on achievement of both financial performance and employee retention targets”.
 Founded in 2013, 3D Hubs says it has facilitated the production of over 6 million custom parts and products through its digital platform to date.
 The company was backed by Balderton Capital, EQT Ventures, Endeit Capital, Hearst Ventures, DOEN Participaties, Future Shape, and Rockstart, among others.
 Tech.eu  paid a visit to the company back in early 2015 after they made our list of  10 European 3D printing startups to watch – quite a journey.
 “The addition of 3D Hubs provides Protolabs a platform to evolve our service model to provide unprecedented manufacturing flexibility to our customers,” said Rob Bodor, Protolabs’ current VP and GM of the Americas and incoming President and CEO.
 “Our combined organizations will provide the market an industry-leading digital manufacturing solution to serve their needs from idea to prototype to full end-use part production. Together we can fulfill nearly every custom manufacturing need across the product life cycle,” he added.
title: 定制零件制造商Protolabs收购3D Hubs，这是一项在线按需服务，可访问约240个制造合作伙伴，现金和股票价格最高为3.3亿美元
title_en: Custom parts manufacturer Protolabs buys 3D Hubs, an online on-demand service providing access to ~240 manufacturing partners, for up to $330M in cash and stock

note:
从386开始，x86处理器支持分页，该分页使用页表将虚拟地址页映射到物理地址页。该映射由操作系统控制，该操作系统为用户应用程序提供了连续的虚拟内存空间，并隔离了不同进程的内存空间。
 页表位于主内存中，因此需要一个缓存（TLB：转换后备缓冲区）以达到可接受的性能。当执行虚拟到物理地址的转换时，TLB将虚拟页面映射到物理页面，并且通常与L1缓存并行查找。对于x86，如果出现TLB丢失，则处理器将“遍历”内存中的页表。其他一些体系结构则引发异常，并要求操作系统将所需的条目加载到TLB中。
 x86体系结构指定TLB与内存访问（即页面表）不相关或不按顺序排列，并且要求在对页面表进行任何更改后刷新相关的TLB条目（或整个TLB）。如果该条目位于TLB中，则无法使之无效将导致处理器使用TLB中的陈旧条目，或者页面表遍历可能不确定地看到旧页面表条目或新页面表条目。对于乱序的处理器，宽松的一致性要求使处理器可以更轻松地对操作进行重新排序以提高性能。
 但是，真正的处理器实现是否真的以此方式运行，还是某些处理器在实践中提供了更多的一致性保证？一个特别有趣的情况是，当更改一个已知不会在TLB中缓存的页表条目，然后立即将其用于翻译（通过分页浏览）而没有任何无效时，会发生什么情况。真实处理器的页面走动是否比规范要求的连贯性更好（并且任何软件都依赖此规范）？如果分页浏览与内存保持一致，则使用什么机制？
 在本文的其余部分中，我将尝试回答上述一些问题，并评估一堆x86处理器的分页浏览行为。
    分页的基本任务是将虚拟地址映射到物理地址。这以“页面”的粒度发生（x86上默认为4 KB）。这意味着对于32位地址，高20位将被转换（“虚拟页号”映射为“物理页号”），而低12位将保持不变（“页偏移”）。图1显示了一个示例，其中虚拟地址0x12345678映射到0x00abc678，进行了两次内存访问。通过将页表的级别数分别增加到3和4，PAE和64位扩展名允许更大的地址空间。
  当然，如果每个内存访问变为三个访问，则性能损失将是不可接受的。 TLB（图2）是翻译的高速缓存，用于存储以前的页面遍历的结果，该结果将虚拟页码直接映射到物理页码，而无需读取TLB命中的页表。 
只要有缓存，就存在缓存一致性的问题。如果页面表条目被更改，TLB中的相应条目是否会自动更新或无效，或者软件是否需要显式使TLB无效或刷新？在x86（以及我能想到的所有其他体系结构）中，TLB是不一致的。非相干TLB的行为非常直观：如果TLB中缓存了一个陈旧的条目，则转换将使用TLB中的陈旧的条目。但是，如果在TLB中没有缓存过时的条目，则转换需要页表遍历。该页表的走行是否与内存访问一致？这是Intel和AMD的手册中所说的：
  处理器可以缓存预取和访问所需要的转换，这些转换是推测执行的结果，而实际上不会在执行的代码路径中发生。
  …处理器有可能在步骤2中将虚拟页A的页表更新到[N页]之前从物理页M中预取数据…当从页表更新之外预取指令时，可能会发生类似的行为指令。为避免此问题，软件必须在页表更新后立即使用INVLPG或MOV CR3指令…
 英特尔和AMD手册均声明，即使旧条目未缓存在TLB中，也不会无效，但pagewalk仍然可以看到旧条目。也就是说，在下面的伪代码中，第二条指令（装入）可以不确定地使用旧映射或新映射，并且它们之间必须存在无效或TLB刷新，以确保新页表项对用户可见。第二条指令。 mov [页表]，new_mapping mov eax，[使用更新的映射的线性地址]
 有趣的是，Pentium 4规范更新中提到了非连贯的分页浏览行为是一种错误（N94，NoFix），即使所谓的错误行为仍然是“正确的”。
 在本文中，我使用“ TLB一致性”来指代内存中的页表与TLB内容（影响TLB命中）之间的一致性，而“ pagewalk一致性”指的是页游与页面表修改之间的一致性（影响TLB未命中）。
  即使指令集规范不能保证Pagewalk的一致性，也有OS代码依靠它来保证正确性。通过Me的Windows 95中有内核代码，可修改页面表映射而不会使其失效，并且如果页面漫游不连贯，则会导致随机崩溃。 
这使我认为也许真正的处理器确实提供了页面漫游的一致性（或者Windows 95通过Me容易崩溃，嗯……）
   第一个设计决策是TLB内容是否与内存访问（页表修改）保持一致。这不是必需的，真正的处理器也不提供它。
 第二个设计决策是页表遍历是否与内存访问一致。指令集规范不需要它，但是无论如何，许多处理器选择提供一致性。
 对于确实提供一致性的处理器，有两种机制可以提供一致性：在所有较早的内存指令完成后，所有分页操作都可以非推测性地执行。或者，为了获得更高的性能，可以使用一种机制来推测性地执行分页查询，如果处理器稍后检测到该分页查询与较早的存储冲突时，该机制可以检测并重试该分页查询。
 分页走行行为的设计空间可分为三类：非相干分页走行，以非推测方式进行的连贯分页走行和以“检测并重试”以推测方式进行的相干分页走行。
   Intel Pentium Pro（6、1、9）Intel Pentium 4 Northwood（15、2、9）Intel Sandy Bridge（6、42、7）Intel Ivy Bridge（6、58、9）Intel Haswell（6、60、3） AMD K7雷鸟（6、4、2）AMD K7纯种马（6、8、1）AMD Phenom（16、2、3）AMD Phenom II（16、10、0）
  Intel Atom Silvermont（6、55、3）通过C3“ Samuel 2”（CentaurHauls 6、7、3） 
AMD推土机（21、1、2）AMD打桩机（21、2、0）AMD Zen 2（23、113、0）Ryzen 5 3600X
  具有影子页表的VirtualBox的行为就像一个连贯的TLB（而不仅仅是连贯的分页浏览）。在没有TLB无效的情况下，页面表的更新总是立即可见。但是，与每个简单的存储指令中的一个或几个相比，提供这种一致性的代价是每次页表更新大约需要60,000个时钟周期。
 虚拟机具有两种转换级别：客户机虚拟到客户机物理（这也是主机虚拟地址），然后是通常的虚拟到主机物理翻译，具有两组页面表，一组由客户机OS管理，一组由客户机OS管理。主机操作系统。没有嵌套分页的虚拟机通常实现影子页面表，其中CPU使用“影子”页面表将客户虚拟地址直接映射到主机物理地址。 VM监视来宾OS的页面表中的更改，并作为响应更新影子页面表。通常，来宾页表被标记为只读，因此所有写操作都会立即捕获到要处理的VM中。
   测试TLB和pagewalk的一致性需要更改页表映射并观察其行为和时间。该测试需要访问页表所需的内核模式代码。还需要创建一种机制，以从用户模式快速修改页表。
 该测试有两种变体，一种用于测试TLB和页面漫游的一致性（功能），另一种基于时间的测试用于测试是否可以以推测的方式进行页面漫游，以及是否因误导而受到惩罚。这些测试然后在多个系统上运行。
  页表位于物理地址空间中，由操作系统控制，通常无法从用户模式访问。要访问页表，需要了解页表在（物理）内存中的位置，以及一种读写物理内存的方法。
 当前进程使用的页表存储在控制寄存器CR3中。具有从CR3直接读取（使用MOV指令）的特权，因此不能在用户模式下进行。 Linux似乎没有提供替代方法来读取进程的CR3，因此我编写了一个简短的内核模块，该模块仅报告当前CR3值（大概对当前进程有效）。 
访问物理内存更容易，因为Linux已经提供了一个/ dev / mem字符设备，该设备可以读写物理内存（只要内核未使用STRICT_DEVMEM进行编译）。知道页表的位置并具有读取/写入任意物理内存的机制，这使我能够编写可以遍历和修改页表的分页程序。根据使用的分页类型，有不同的例程（两级32位x86，三级32位PAE和四级48位x86-64）。
 但是，因为/ dev / mem是通过文件读/写操作访问的，并且需要切换到内核模式，所以它太慢了。微基准测试需要一种仅用一条指令即可快速更新页表条目的方法。
  通过使用/ dev / mem设置页表来完成快速页表更新，以便虚拟内存空间的区域映射到其他数组的页表。操作系统也经常使用类似的东西。这允许用户模式代码（启用分页）直接用一条指令更新页表，而无需切换到内核模式，也无需禁用分页。图3显示了页表设置。更改了控制pt_window数组映射的页表（使用慢速的/ dev / mem），以便对pt_array的访问实际上访问了bigbuf的页表，从而可以快速修改bigbuf的页表映射。
 在进程退出时，Linux会检查该进程的页表的一致性，因此，微基准测试会备份它修改的任何页表项，并在退出前将其恢复。
      TLB和pagewalk一致性关系到修改页表映射然后在没有中间无效的情况下使用时处理器的行为。我们可以通过将映射更改为指向具有已知内容的两个页面之一来进行测量，因此加载的结果指示实际使用了哪个页面映射。将所有映射指向仅两页还有一个额外的优势，即仅使用8 KB的（VIPT或PIPT）L1缓存占用空间，可以实现更好的时序测量，同时减少来自数据缓存未命中的干扰。
 该测试以随机循环顺序访问页面，以击败预取并极有可能生成TLB未命中。默认情况下，所有页面都指向包含数字1的页面（“页面1”）。对于每个页面，映射都会更改为指向包含数字2的不同页面（“页面2”），然后立即由负载使用。立即将其更改回指向第1页，并执行了另一个加载，因此页表仅非常简短地指向第2页。
 * p_thispte = map_pte2; //更改映射n = *（unsigned int *）p_array; //首次访问：n始终为1或2。lcounts [n + 1] ++; //计算n的出现* p_thispte = map_pte1; //将映射改回n = *（unsigned int *）p_array; //二次访问lcounts [n-1] ++; 
在完全一致的系统中，第一个负载将从新更改的映射中读取2，第二个负载将在还原映射后立即读取1。 如果TLB不相干，则第二个负载仍将看到2，因为第一个负载已将映射放入第二个负载重用的TLB中。 如果分页浏览不连贯，则第一次加载可以（但不一定）为1，因为分页浏览是推测性地发生在页表更新之前的。
note_en:
Since the 386, x86 processors have supported paging, which uses a page table to map virtual address pages to physical address pages. This mapping is controlled by the operating system, which gives user applications a contiguous virtual memory space, and isolates the memory spaces of different processes.
 Page tables are located in main memory, so a cache (TLB: Translation Lookaside Buffer) is needed for acceptable performance. When doing virtual to physical address translations, the TLB maps virtual pages to physical pages, and is typically looked up in parallel with the L1 cache. For x86, the processor “walks” the page tables in memory if there is a TLB miss. Some other architectures throw an exception and ask the OS to load the required entry into the TLB.
 The x86 architecture specifies that the TLB is  not coherent or ordered with memory accesses (i.e., page tables), and requires that the relevant TLB entry (or the entire TLB) be flushed after any changes to the page tables. Failing to invalidate would cause the processor to use the stale entry in the TLB if the entry is in the TLB, or a page table walk could non-deterministically see either the old or new page table entry. With out-of-order processors, relaxing coherence requirements allows the processor to more easily reorder operations for more performance.
 But do real processor implementations really behave this way, or do some processors provide more coherence guarantees in practice? One particular interesting case concerns what happens when a page table entry that is known not to be cached in the TLB is changed, then immediately used for a translation (via a pagewalk) without any invalidations. Are real processors’ pagewalks more coherent than required by the specification (and does any software rely on this)? And if pagewalks are coherent with memory, what mechanism is used?
 In the rest of this post, I’ll attempt answer some of the above questions, and measure the pagewalking behaviour of a bunch of x86 processors.
    The basic task of paging is to map virtual addresses to physical addresses. This occurs at the granularity of a “page” (default 4 KB on x86). This means that for a 32-bit address, the upper 20 bits are translated (“virtual page number” mapped to “physical page number”), while the lower 12 are unchanged (“page offset”). Figure 1 shows an example where the virtual address 0x12345678 is mapped to 0x00abc678, doing two memory accesses. PAE and 64-bit extensions allow larger address spaces by increasing the number of levels of page tables, to 3 and 4, respectively.
  Of course, if every memory access became three accesses, the performance penalty would not be acceptable. A TLB (Figure 2) is a cache of translations that stores the result of previous pagewalks, which directly maps virtual page numbers to physical page numbers without reading the page tables on a TLB hit.
    Whenever there are caches, there is the problem of cache coherence. If a page table entry is changed, would the corresponding entry in the TLB be automatically updated or invalidated, or would software need to explicitly invalidate or flush the TLB? In x86 (and all other architectures I can think of), the TLB is not coherent. The behaviour of non-coherent TLBs is fairly intuitive: if there is a stale entry cached in the TLB, a translation would use the stale entry in the TLB. But if there is no stale entry cached in the TLB, a translation requires a page table walk. Is this page table walk coherent with memory accesses? Here is what Intel’s and AMD’s manuals say about it:
  The processor may cache translations required for prefetches and for accesses that are a result of speculative execution that would never actually occur in the executed code path.
  …it is possible for the processor to prefetch the data from physical-page M before the page table for virtual-page A is updated in Step 2 [to page N] … Similar behavior can occur when instructions are prefetched from beyond the page table update instruction. To prevent this problem, software must use an INVLPG or MOV CR3 instruction immediately after the page-table update…
 Both Intel and AMD manuals state that without invalidation, even if the old entry is not cached in the TLB, the pagewalk may still see the old entry. That is, in the following pseudocode, the second instruction (load) can non-deterministically use either the old mapping or new mapping, and that there must be an invalidation or TLB flush in between to guarantee the new page table entry is visible by the second instruction.   mov [page table], new_mapping mov eax, [linear address using updated mapping]
 Interestingly, non-coherent pagewalk behaviour is mentioned in the Pentium 4  specification update as an erratum ( N94, NoFix) even though the supposedly-erroneous behaviour is still “correct”.
 In this post, I use “TLB coherence” to refer to coherence between page tables in memory and the TLB contents (affecting TLB hits), and “pagewalk coherence” for the coherence between pagewalks and page table modifications (affecting TLB misses).
  Even though the instruction set specification does not guarantee pagewalk coherence, there is OS code that relies on it for correctness. There is kernel code in Windows 95 through Me  that modifies page table mappings without invalidation, and this causes random crashes if pagewalks are not coherent.
 This leads me to think that perhaps real processors do provide pagewalk coherence (or that Windows 95 through Me were crash-prone, hmm…)
   The first design decision is whether TLB contents are coherent with memory accesses (page table modifications). This is not required, and real processors do not provide it.
 The second design decision is whether page table walks are coherent with memory accesses. The instruction set specifications do not require it, but many processors choose to provide coherence anyway.
 For processors that do provide coherence, there are two classes of mechanisms to provide it: All pagewalks could be performed non-speculatively, after all earlier memory instructions are completed. Or, for more performance, pagewalks could be performed speculatively, with a mechanism to detect and retry the pagewalk if the processor later detects that the pagewalk conflicted with an earlier store to memory.
 The design space of pagewalk behaviours can be classified into three categories: Non-coherent pagewalks, coherent pagewalks done non-speculatively, and coherent pagewalks done speculatively with detect-and-retry.
   Intel Pentium Pro (6, 1, 9) Intel Pentium 4 Northwood (15, 2, 9) Intel Sandy Bridge (6, 42, 7) Intel Ivy Bridge (6, 58, 9) Intel Haswell (6, 60, 3) AMD K7 Thunderbird (6, 4, 2) AMD K7 Thoroughbred (6, 8, 1) AMD Phenom (16, 2, 3) AMD Phenom II (16, 10, 0)
  Intel Atom Silvermont (6, 55, 3) Via C3 “Samuel 2” (CentaurHauls 6, 7, 3)
 AMD Bulldozer (21, 1, 2) AMD Piledriver (21, 2, 0) AMD Zen 2 (23, 113, 0) Ryzen 5 3600X
  VirtualBox with shadow page tables behaves as a coherent TLB (not merely coherent pagewalks). Updates to page tables are always immediately visible without TLB invalidation. But the cost of providing this coherence is around 60,000 clock cycles per page table update, compared to one or a few for a simple store instruction.
 Virtual machines have two levels of translation: Guest virtual to guest physical (which is also the host virtual address), then the usual host virtual to host physical translation, with two sets of page tables, one managed by the guest OS, one managed by the host OS. Virtual machines without nested paging usually implement  shadow page tables, where the CPU uses “shadow” page tables that directly map guest virtual addresses to host physical addresses. The VM monitors changes in the guest OS’s page tables and updates the shadow page tables in response. Often the guest page tables are marked as read-only so all writes immediately trap into the VM to be handled.
   Testing for TLB and pagewalk coherence requires changing page table mappings and observing their behaviour and timing. The test needed kernel-mode code needed to gain access to the page tables. It also needed to create a mechanism for modifying the page tables quickly from user mode.
 There were two variations of the test, one to test for TLB and pagewalk coherence (functionality), and another timing-based test to test whether pagewalks can occur speculatively and whether there is a penalty for misspeculations. These tests were then run on multiple systems.
  Page tables are located in physical address space, is controlled by the OS, and normally inaccessible from user mode. Gaining access to the page tables requires that we know where the page tables are located in (physical) memory, and a method to read and write to physical memory.
 The page tables used by the current process is stored in control register CR3. Directly reading from CR3 (using a MOV instruction) is privileged, so can’t be done in user mode. Linux does not appear to provide an alternative mechanism to read a process’s CR3, so I wrote a short kernel module that simply reports the current CR3 value (presumably valid for the current process).
 Accessing physical memory is easier, as Linux already provides a /dev/mem character device that can read and write to physical memory (as long as the kernel was not compiled with STRICT_DEVMEM). Knowing the location of the page tables and having a mechanism to read/write arbitrary physical memory allows me to write pagewalking routines that can traverse and modify the page tables. There are different routines depending on the type of paging in use (two-level 32-bit x86, three-level 32-bit PAE, and four-level 48-bit x86-64).
 However, since /dev/mem is accessed with file read/write operations and requires a switch into kernel mode, it is much too slow. A microbenchmark needs a method to update a page table entry quickly with just one instruction.
  Fast page table updates is done by using /dev/mem to set up the page tables so that a region of the virtual memory space maps to the page tables for a different array. Something similar is often used by OSes as well. This allows user-mode code (with paging enabled) to directly update page tables with a single instruction, without switching to kernel mode, and without disabling paging. Figure 3 shows the page table setup. The page tables that control the mappings for the  pt_window array are changed (using slow /dev/mem) so that accesses to  pt_array actually access the page tables for  bigbuf, allowing fast modification of  bigbuf‘s page table mappings.
 Linux checks the consistency of a process’s page tables when the process exits, so the microbenchmark backs up any of the page tables entries it modifies and restores them before exiting.
      TLB and pagewalk coherence concerns the processor’s behaviour when a page table mapping is modified and then used without an intervening invalidation. We can measure this by changing the mappings to point to one of two pages with known contents, so the result of a load indicates which page mapping was actually used. Pointing all of the mappings to just two pages has the additional advantage of using only 8 KB of (VIPT or PIPT) L1 cache footprint, allowing better timing measurements with less interference from data cache misses.
 The test accesses pages in a random cyclic ordering, to defeat prefetching and generate TLB misses with high probability. All pages point to a page containing the number 1 (“page 1”) by default. For each page, the mapping is changed to point to a different page containing the number 2 (“page 2”), then immediately used by a load. This is immediately changed back to point to page 1, and another load performed, so the page tables point to page 2 only very briefly.
 *p_thispte = map_pte2; // Change mappingn = *(unsigned int*)p_array; // First access: n is always 1 or 2.lcounts[n+1]++; // Count occurrences of n*p_thispte = map_pte1; // Change mapping backn = *(unsigned int*)p_array; // Second accesslcounts[n-1]++;
 In a fully-coherent system, the first load will read a 2 from the newly-changed mapping, and the second load will read a 1 right after the mapping is reverted. If the TLB is not coherent, the second load will still see 2 because the first load has already placed the mapping in the TLB which the second load reuses. If the pagewalks are not coherent, the first load can (but doesn’t necessarily) see 1, because the pagewalk speculatively occurred before the page table update.
title: x86处理器中的TLB和Pagewalk一致性（2015）
title_en: TLB and Pagewalk Coherence in x86 Processors (2015)

 当您适应大流行时，您是否看到有关收入增长，保留或其他动量的“绿芽”？是的，如上所述。
 在过去一个月左右的时间里，给您带来希望的时刻是什么？这可以是专业的，个人的或两者的结合。我们始终需要保持积极态度，不要夸大这种流行病。会过去的。
   一般来说，您最感兴趣的投资趋势是什么？鉴于我们有限的地域范围（我们仅在罗马尼亚投资），我们必须采取通用的方法并考虑许多垂直领域和趋势。举个例子，我们正在研究初创公司将已被广泛采用的技术应用到利基领域：将计算机视觉应用于特定作物（农业技术）或应用于店内客户行为（martech）；生物统计数据（通过可穿戴设备收集的数据）应用于与单个人相反的群体互动；超轻型区块链分类帐应用于智能建筑...从另一个投资角度来看，我们正在寻求投资所谓的“创新基础设施”，例如构建API的初创公司-我们认为罗马尼亚的API不够完善。
 您最新，最激动人心的投资是什么？我们签署的最后一个条款表是与一家旨在构建技术以帮助企业级公司更好地管理其软件许可证的初创公司有关的。超级怪异的团队，在该领域拥有深厚的专业知识，为客户创造了很多价值。
 是否有您希望在行业中看到但没有的初创公司？现在有哪些被忽视的机会？我想找一些优秀的团队来尝试简化游戏中的支付（在支付和游戏的交集处进行构建），或致力于“服务即灌溉”（农业技术），或为能源开发NASDAQ。
 一般来说，您在下一笔投资中寻找什么？我正在寻找既有能力又有勇气的创始人。这样的人将敢于解决大问题，并有机会成功解决它。 
哪些领域要么饱和，要么在这一点上很难竞争新的创业公司？您还警惕或关注其他哪些类型的产品/服务？我已经看到太多的初创公司正在构建可帮助人们找到停车位的应用程序，太多了没人需要的市场，太多了用于营销的现成技术，太多了CRM和ERP。
 一般而言，相对于其他启动中心（或其他地方），您有多少专注于投资本地生态系统？超过50％？减？我们仅在罗马尼亚投资-100％致力于当地生态系统。
 您所在城市和地区的哪些行业似乎处于蓬勃发展的位置，或者不是长期的？您对哪些公司感到兴奋（无论您是否投资于公司），哪些创始人？我认为，鉴于罗马尼亚的自然潜力，农业技术有很大的机会。这个空间还没有得到充分的服务。否则，网络安全，企业软件和金融科技都可以很好地代表。从我们将近20家初创公司的投资组合中，CODA使托管服务提供商具有网络安全技能。人类正在建立合成媒体技术的中心。机械正在使农业设备彼此对话； Tokinomo正在从货架上收集和分析数据（店内营销）； BunnyShell正在开发下一代云技术，并使任何人都可以轻松地单击三下以设置服务器。
 其他城市的投资者应如何考虑您城市的总体投资环境和机会？罗马尼亚的启动生态系统还很年轻，三年前在欧洲投资基金的支持下建立了第一批本地风险投资基金。然而，罗马尼亚拥有三家独角兽公司和许多其他有前途的初创公司。庞大的技术人才库，广泛使用的宽带接入以及低廉的经商和生活成本使罗马尼亚成为一个值得关注的市场。
 您是否希望在未来几年内看到更多来自主要城市以外地区的创始人，由于流行病和持续的担忧以及偏远地区工作的吸引力，创业中心使人员流失？我希望大城市的创始人能够留在大城市，因为为一家初创公司成立并工作并不意味着在偏远的海滩上用笔记本电脑编写代码。在苦苦寻找产品市场契合度的过程中，创始人需要与企业洽谈业务，拜访合作伙伴，签订合同，参加活动，与同行会面，进行调查，制作原型以及其他1000项无法完全使用Zoom进行的工作。科技行业和新兴国家和世界其他地区一样受到大流行的打击。就像世界其他地区一样，它们将从2020年3月开始生存，适应并大部分恢复正常的互动。
 由于COVID-19，您所投资的哪个细分市场显得更弱或更受消费者和商业行为的潜在变化的影响？在这些空前的时期，初创企业可能会利用哪些机会？不幸的是，我看到城市交通应用程序受到大流行的限制。此外，与餐馆，酒店和常规活动有关的任何事物都受到严重影响。我们在这些垂直领域投资了初创公司，并在大流行的最糟糕时期竭尽所能来帮助他们。
 COVID-19如何影响您的投资策略？您的投资组合中创始人最大的担忧是什么？您现在对投资组合中的初创企业有何建议？与2019年相比，我们的基金在2020年的投资数量减少了20％，交易总价值减少了40％;因此，COVID的影响很大。同时，就基金业绩而言，2020年是丰收的一年，我们投资组合中的公司与外部投资者进行了新一轮投资，提高了估值，并显示出良好的回报。 2020年上半年专门用于损害控制措施并为投资组合公司提供支持，但情况在年底前发生了变化，上一季度的新交易活跃（高于2019年第四季度）。由风投支持的初创公司有投资者在艰难时期求助，并在需要时从支持和额外资金中受益。对于其他创业公司来说，事情要困难得多。 
当您适应大流行时，您是否看到有关收入增长，保留或其他动量的“绿芽”？大流行开始的那一刻，我们注意到的第一件事就是生产力达到顶峰。在家里强制性安置的几个月中，从事原型设计的早期创业公司投入了额外的时间并提高了速度。人员保留很好，人们专注，有积极进取的精神和实现事情的普遍愿望。确实，一些初创公司报告说销售立即增长，例如Tokinomo的机器人取代了超市中的人类促销员。
 在过去一个月左右的时间里，给您带来希望的时刻是什么？这可以是专业的，个人的或两者的结合。 2020年的一大亮点是我与朋友们一起在希腊群岛上乘着宏伟的游艇航行的那一周。充电时刻使我在今年余下的时间里都得到了提振。下一个兴高采烈的时刻是在12月进行的A系列投资，这些投资提高了我们基金的业绩。
   一般来说，您最感兴趣的投资趋势是什么？初创企业与罗马尼亚和更广泛的东欧创始人一起创建世界领先的知识产权
  一般来说，您在下一笔投资中寻找什么？坚定的，坚定的创始人对他们计划通过创新知识产权在全球范围内颠覆的领域有深刻的了解。
 哪些领域要么饱和，要么在这一点上很难竞争新的创业公司？您还警惕或关注其他哪些类型的产品/服务？我认为太多的人试图启动平台，却没有太多了解在没有主要差异的情况下启动平台的难度。如果没有其他不公平的优势来启动这种平台，则通过其他数字营销平台获得客户的成本将非常昂贵。
 一般而言，相对于其他启动中心（或其他地方），您有多少专注于投资本地生态系统？超过50％？减？我们非常关注罗马尼亚的创始人-但他们可以在世界任何地方居住和创立。我们对许多罗马尼亚创始人创办的美国公司进行了投资。
 您所在城市和地区的哪些行业似乎处于蓬勃发展的位置，或者不是长期的？您对哪些公司感到兴奋（无论您是否投资于公司），哪些创始人？我认为罗马尼亚在赢得网络安全和企业软件以及基于AI的引擎方面处于非常有利的位置。我对我们产品组合中的pentest-tools.com，deepstash.com，uniapply.com以及我们产品组合中不存在的Fintech OS和TypingDNA感到非常兴奋。 
其他城市的投资者应如何考虑您城市的总体投资环境和机会？布加勒斯特是一个欣欣向荣的生态系统，拥有大量的机会可以进行全球扩张。
 您是否希望在未来几年内看到更多来自主要城市以外地区的创始人，由于流行病和持续的担忧以及偏远地区工作的吸引力，创业中心使人员流失？实际上，我们已经看到来自罗马尼亚较小城市的创始人激增。我们是创新实验室预加速器的创始合作伙伴，它在全国范围内占有一席之地，并且我们看到越来越多的学生有兴趣成为罗马尼亚各地的创始者。
 由于COVID-19，您所投资的哪个细分市场显得更弱或更受消费者和商业行为的潜在变化的影响？在这些空前的时期，初创企业可能会利用哪些机会？出行解决方案受到影响，本地参与者正在输给更大的参与者，例如Lime。
 COVID-19如何影响您的投资策略？您的投资组合中创始人最大的担忧是什么？您现在对投资组合中的初创企业有何建议？大流行病推迟了很多投资，但我们在年底将其关闭。创始人最大的担忧是，他们作为一家初创公司吸引技术人才的杠杆越来越少。问题在于，技术人员现在可以为世界上任何一家公司工作，这使他们的薪水/薪水飞涨。
 当您适应大流行时，您是否看到有关收入增长，保留或其他动量的“绿芽”？是的，我们已经看到了-其中一些受益于人们待在家里并拥有更多时间。
 在过去一个月左右的时间里，给您带来希望的时刻是什么？这可以是专业的，个人的或两者的结合。在罗马尼亚，疫苗的引入和人们的疫苗接种速度并不是最快，但也不是最慢。在线约会约会平台已启动并正在运行，人们正在使用它！
   一般来说，您最感兴趣的投资趋势是什么？健康与流行病危机“帮助”的领域正处于转型和增长的边缘。随着客户和监管机构的开放，软件和硬件的就绪状态正在迅速发展。 
您最新，最激动人心的投资是什么？送餐服务。仍然是第0天，在消费者服务和业务增长方面将有巨大的机遇：食物，现成的饭菜，方便食品，杂货等。
 是否有您希望在行业中看到但没有的初创公司？现在有哪些被忽视的机会？尽管初创企业数量相对较多，但B2C和B2B2C金融技术领域仍有增长空间。
 一般而言，相对于其他启动中心（或其他地方），您有多少专注于投资本地生态系统？超过50％？减？当我们从技术角度看人才库时，布加勒斯特和罗马尼亚总体上具有巨大潜力，并且是开始扩大区域和全球规模的好地方。
 其他城市的投资者应如何考虑您城市的总体投资环境和机会？未来的好去处：近三年来，一流的基础设施（互联网成本和速度，集线器数量），人才库和投资交易数量的增加。
 您是否希望在未来几年内看到更多来自主要城市以外地区的创始人，由于流行病和持续的担忧以及偏远地区工作的吸引力，创业中心使人员流失？我希望看到其他地区的创始人人数有所增长，但不会激增。我认为这对生态系统来说是一件好事。
 由于COVID-19，您所投资的哪个细分市场显得更弱或更受消费者和商业行为的潜在变化的影响？在这些空前的时期，初创企业可能会利用哪些机会？旅行既暴露无遗，同时也为新创业公司带来了巨大的潜力。消费者将有一个“复仇旅行”期，但他们会寻找不同的东西，与此同时，商务旅行也将有所不同，这将产生新的做法和行为。
 COVID-19如何影响您的投资策略？您的投资组合中创始人最大的担忧是什么？您现在对投资组合中的初创企业有何建议？我们看到了增长的机会，我们正在分配更多的资金进行投资，并且我们建议初创公司增加投资并更快地发展。 
在过去一个月左右的时间里，给您带来希望的时刻是什么？这可以是专业的，个人的或两者的结合。全球疫苗运动的开始和初步结果。
   一般来说，您最感兴趣的投资趋势是什么？ B2B平台由金融科技ML /自动化/人工智能，SaaS企业软件，网络安全，医疗保健IT，低代码开发环境，对话技术，物流自动化实现
 您最新，最激动人心的投资是什么？ –最新的投资是2021年1月12日宣布的DruidAI。GapMinder领导了250万美元的融资。 –其他2020年令人振奋的新投资或后续行动：TypingDNA，FintechOS，DeepStash，Soleadify，机械加工，创新，Frisbo，Cartloop，XVision
 一般来说，您在下一笔投资中寻找什么？ –阶段：种子或A轮系列–技术：由ML或AI辅助的自动化或对话技术–团队：具有良好的国际扩展记录； –产品：B2B可扩展的国际业务，主要关注B2B平台
 哪些领域要么饱和，要么在这一点上很难竞争新的创业公司？您还警惕或关注其他哪些类型的产品/服务？ CEE中包含的B2C模型（来自美国）的副本往往仅限于小型本地市场，并演变为高度拥挤的环境。罗马尼亚的共享经济公司就是这样的例子。作为风投投资者，单位经济学对我们而言根本没有吸引力。
 一般而言，相对于其他启动中心（或其他地方），您有多少专注于投资本地生态系统？超过50％？减？超过70％
 您所在城市和地区的哪些行业似乎处于蓬勃发展的位置，或者不是长期的？您对哪些公司感到兴奋（无论您是否投资于公司），哪些创始人？ –我们认为的模型将继续蓬勃发展：与B2C模型相比，由自动化/会话技术支持的B2B平台（在ML / AI的辅助下）具有更高的国际化潜力。 –具有较高潜力的垂直行业，我们在上面提到了一些。 –令人振奋的GapMinder投资组合公司：FintechOS，TypingDNA，DeepStash，DruidAI，Soleadify，机械加工，创新，Frisbo，Cartloop，SmartDreamers，XVision等
